# cppcheck_suppressions.txt
# Suppression list for FreeRTOS-OS CPPcheck analysis.
#
# Format: checkId:filepath_pattern:lineNumber
#   - Omit lineNumber to suppress on all lines.
#   - Use * as a wildcard for checkId to suppress all checks on a path.
#
# Run with: cppcheck --suppressions-list=scripts/cppcheck_suppressions.txt ...
#
# ── Rationale ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Third-party / auto-generated code is excluded at the source-list level in
# run_cppcheck.sh, so suppressions here cover only project code patterns that
# are intentional or false-positives inherent to embedded bare-metal C.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


# ── Intentional embedded patterns ─────────────────────────────────────────────

# Infinite loops are the norm for RTOS tasks (while(1) / for(;;))
endlessLoop

# Volatile-qualified peripheral register accesses trigger redundantAssignment
redundantAssignment

# Register bit-field casts from uint32_t to smaller types are intentional
invalidPointerCast

# cppcheck may flag __attribute__((packed)) structs as uninitialized
uninitStructMember

# memset/memcpy on structs with padding fields — safe in this context
memsetClassFloat

# Interrupt handlers use implicit fall-through in switch-case intentionally
implicitFallthrough


# ── MISRA-specific suppressions (documented deviations) ───────────────────────

# Rule 15.5 – A function should have a single point of exit.
# FreeRTOS assert macros use immediate return; accepted as project deviation.
misra-c2012-15.5

# Rule 21.6 – Standard library input/output functions shall not be used.
# ITM/SWO debug printf is used only when CONFIG_DEFAULT_DEBUG_EN=1.
misra-c2012-21.6

# Rule 11.4 – A cast should not be performed between a pointer to object type
# and an integer type. Required for MMIO register address mapping.
misra-c2012-11.4

# Rule 11.6 – A cast shall not be performed between pointer to void and
# arithmetic type. FreeRTOS task parameter passing uses void*.
misra-c2012-11.6

# Rule 2.5 – A project should not contain unused macro definitions.
# CONFIG_* macros from autoconf.h are not all consumed in every TU.
misra-c2012-2.5

# Rule 5.4 – Macro identifiers shall be distinct from any other identifier.
# STM32 HAL header guard names collide; not under our control.
misra-c2012-5.4

# Rule 8.9 – An object should be defined at block scope if its identifier
# only appears in a single function. Static ISR tables must be file-scope.
misra-c2012-8.9


# ── Auto-generated board files ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# These files are machine-generated; only include path matters, not style.
*:*/board/irq_periph_dispatch_generated.c
*:*/board/irq_table_generated.c
*:*/board/irq_hw_init_generated.c

# ── CMSIS / STM32 vendor headers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# cmsis_gcc.h emits a false #error when __ARM_ARCH_PROFILE is not predefined.
# The macro is set by arm-none-eabi-gcc at compile time; cppcheck can't see it.
preprocessorErrorDirective:*/CMSIS_6/*
preprocessorErrorDirective:*/CMSIS/Core/*
